data.title: Aben Gold completes private funding round, raising significant capital.
data.description: In a recent development, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.8%. This marks a significant improvement from the previous rate of 4.2%. The number of new jobs created in the last month alone was an impressive 250,000. Additionally, the average hourly earnings for workers have seen a notable increase of 3.5% compared to last year. These figures indicate a strong and growing economy, with more people finding employment and experiencing wage growth.

data.title: Genesis introduces off-road X Skorpio concept, demonstrating real-world driving in the UAE desert; part of new concept car strategy.
data.description: Genesis has introduced an off-road concept car designed for challenging terrain, showcasing its driving capabilities in the Rub' al Khali desert in the UAE. The world premiere featured concept models tailored for off-road adventures, such as the GV60 Outdoors Concept and GV70 Outdoors. The event demonstrated the vehicle's performance in real-world conditions, emphasizing its suitability for demanding terrains.

data.title: Osmose acquires Mainswest Pty Ltd and Centillion Solutions Pty Ltd to expand underground services in Australia.
data.description: In Atlanta, Osmose has recently acquired Mainswest Pty Ltd and the assets of Centillion Solutions Pty Ltd to enhance its underground and technical services in Australia. The acquisition aims to strengthen Osmose's capabilities in the region. The move signifies a strategic expansion as Osmose looks to capitalize on the growing market trends. The acquisition of Mainswest Pty Ltd and Centillion Solutions Pty Ltd will enable Osmose to provide a wider range of services in Australia, further solidifying its position in the sector. This acquisition marks a significant development for Osmose as it continues to bolster its presence globally.

data.title: First Busey Corporation announces 2025 Q4 earnings: Net income $60.8M, Diluted EPS $0.63, Net interest margin 3.71%, ROAA 1.32%, ROATCE 12.59% (Message from CEO included). CEO transition details disclosed. Paraphrased full story available on Benzinga.com.
data.description: First Busey Corporation announced their 2025 fourth quarter earnings with a net income of $60.8 million and a diluted EPS of $0.63. Their net interest margin increased to 3.71%, and they reported an ROAA of 1.32% and ROATCE of 12.59%. The company highlighted a record quarter for wealth management fee income and revealed a strategic reduction in brokered and high-cost deposits. The Chairman, President, and CEO emphasized strong performance, improved profitability, and successful merger integration in the past year. Adjusted net income for the fourth quarter was $65.2 million, or $0.68 per diluted common share. The Board announced executive changes, separation of a key figure, and new appointments. Financial results showed a significant increase in net income compared to the previous year's fourth quarter. The company reported stable loan balances, intentional deposit runoff, and a strong capital position for navigating economic scenarios in 2026. Van A. Dukeman to serve as President of First Busey Corporation and CEO of Busey Bank. Noninterest income, including wealth management fees and payments technology solutions, showed positive growth. Overall, the company is well-positioned for the upcoming year due to its strong financial performance and disciplined risk management practices.

data.title: First Community Bankshares reported Q4 2025 net income of $12.46M and $48.79M for the year. Quarterly dividend declared $0.31/share. Total assets $3.26B, annual ROA 1.52%, ROE 9.64%, and ROA for Q4 2025 was 1.53%. Non-performing assets were $14.15M, and book value per share on Dec 31, 2025, was $27.30, decreasing $1.43 from 2024.
data.description: First Community Bankshares, Inc. reported their unaudited financial results for Q4 2025, with a net income of $12.46 million and $48.79 million for the full year of 2025. The company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.31 per common share and completed the acquisition of Hometown Bancshares, Inc. Hometown had total assets of approximately $415 million at the end of 2025. The company's net interest margin for Q4 2025 was 4.53%, with total non-performing assets of $14.15 million. Consolidated assets totaled $3.26 billion, with total deposits decreasing by $5.92 million. The company also provided various non-GAAP financial measures in addition to their GAAP financial statements. The company's return on average assets was 1.52% for the full year of 2025, with a return on average common equity of 9.64%. Net income decreased from the same period in 2024, largely due to increased noninterest expenses, primarily from merger costs related to the acquisition. The company emphasized its capital management philosophy, intending to return excess capital to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. They did not repurchase any common shares in Q4 2025.

data.title: Stride Inc's (LRN) stock surges after Q2 earnings beat expectations: Revenue $631.26M, EPS $2.50; Enrollments up 7.8%.
data.description: Shares of Stride Inc (NYSE: LRN) surged in after-hours trading following the company's second-quarter fiscal 2026 results, which surpassed expectations. LRN stock displayed strong momentum with Q2 revenue hitting $631.26 million, exceeding the estimated $627.94 million, and Q2 adjusted EPS reaching $2.50, surpassing the anticipated $2.08. Furthermore, second-quarter enrollments rose by 7.8% year-over-year to 248,500, with Career Learning enrollments experiencing a significant increase of 17.6% to 111,500. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nonprofit RenterCertification.ORG launches renter education in 1,429 US correctional facilities, with 217 more in process, reaching over 1 million inmates.
data.description: RenterCertification.ORG, a nonprofit organization dedicated to re-entry readiness and housing stability, has introduced its renter education program in 1,429 correctional facilities nationwide. An additional 217 facilities are in the process of activating the program. This initiative aims to provide education opportunities for over one million incarcerated individuals.

data.description: On January 27, 2026, an announcement was made about the distribution for shareholders of Cohen & Steers Closed-End Opportunity Fund, Inc. (NYSE: FOF). The distribution is scheduled to be paid on January 30, 2026. This communication details the sources of the upcoming distribution, as well as cumulative distributions made during the fiscal period. Shareholders of the Fund can expect to receive further details on these distributions and their sources.

data.title: Stock market hits highs with Nasdaq leading and S&P 500 hitting a high. Tesla, Microsoft, Meta in focus with Fed's Powell.
data.description: Stocks experienced significant upward trends as the Nasdaq showed leadership and the S&P 500 reached a peak. However, attention is on Tesla, Microsoft, Meta, and Federal Reserve Chairman Powell. Dow Jones Futures soared as the stock market achieved new highs. Tesla, Meta, Microsoft, and insights from Powell are anticipated to impact the market in the upcoming session.

data.title: Brazilian equities are surging in 2026, with iShares MSCI Brazil ETF (EWZ) up 20% month-to-date, outperforming SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) by 17 percentage points. EWZ ranks as the third best U.S.-listed country ETF, signaling a potential technical breakout. During the last commodity supercycle, Brazilian equities outpaced S&P 500 by over 1,000%, highlighting their leverage to resource-driven markets.
data.description: Brazilian equities are experiencing a strong resurgence in early 2026 due to surging commodity prices, a weakening U.S. dollar, and a shift towards emerging markets. The iShares MSCI Brazil ETF (NYSE: EWZ) has risen by approximately 20% month-to-date, significantly outperforming the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSE: SPY) which has only gained around 3% in the same period. This 17-percentage-point difference represents the strongest one-month outperformance by EWZ against U.S. equities in over four years. The EWZ is currently the third best-performing U.S.-listed country ETF in the past month, following only the iShares MSCI Peru and Global Exposure ETF (NYSE: EPU) up by 26%, and the iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (NYSE: EWY). The EWZ/SPY spread has broken above its long-term downtrend, indicating a potential technical breakout that could attract more investment into Brazilian equities, historically known for their leverage in resource-driven bull markets. In the last major commodity supercycle from October 2002 to May 2008, Brazilian equities outpaced the S&P 500 by over 1,000%. This growth trend is expected to continue, as experts predict further gains for Brazilian equities. For further details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: International Tower Hill Mines closes US$115M equity financing, issuing 33,672,000 common shares.
data.description: In Vancouver, BC, on January 27, 2026, International Tower Hill Mines Ltd. disclosed the completion of an increased public offering. The offering involved 33,672,000 common shares, with no par value, of the Company. The shares were traded on both TSX under the ticker symbol ITH and on NYSE American as THM.
